summary: The Health IT End-Users Alliance supports the intent to reduce regulatory burden but expresses concern that proposed deregulatory actions will increase burdens on end-users by shifting compliance costs and reducing technology quality. They urge ONC to retain real-world testing and decision support intervention transparency requirements to ensure technology meets clinical needs and supports AI trust and safety.
specific_provisions: Condition and Maintenance of Certification Real World Testing requirement
key_arguments: Removing real-world testing will lead to poorly designed technology, increased workarounds, and wasted resources.
Eliminating DSI transparency removes critical AI oversight, undermining trust and end-user ability to make informed decisions.
End-users face ongoing exclusion from development processes, increasing implementation burdens and costs.
Frequent changes to information blocking rules create compliance challenges and unpredictability for end-users.
recommendations: Retain and strengthen real-world testing requirements in the Certification Program.
Preserve DSI transparency criteria or develop an alternative AI transparency framework with end-user input.
Ensure information blocking exceptions are carefully reviewed to prevent misuse but avoid frequent changes post-finalization.
Engage end-users earlier in policy and technology development to reduce burdens and improve outcomes.
notable_quote: We urge ASTP/ONC to refrain from removing the real-world testing requirement from the Certification Program.
